solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int countNumbers(String l, String r, int b) {
        final int MOD = 1000000007;

        // Helper function to count non-decreasing numbers up to a number in base b
        java.util.function<String, Integer> countUpTo = (s) -> {
            int n = s.length();
            int[][] dp = new int[n + 1][b + 1];
            for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
                for (int j = 0; j <= b; j++) {
                    dp[i][j] = 0;
                }
            }

            for (int i = 0; i <= b; i++) {
                dp[1][i] = 1;
            }

            for (int i = 2; i <= n; i++) {
                for (int d = 0; d <= b; d++) {
                    for (int prev = 0; prev <= d; prev++) {
                        dp[i][d] = (dp[i][d] + dp[i - 1][prev]) % MOD;
                    }
                }
            }

            int res = 0;
            for (int d = 1; d <= b; d++) {
                res = (res + dp[n][d]) % MOD;
            }

            return res;
        };

        // Helper function to count non-decreasing numbers less than a given string in base b
        java.util.function<String, Integer> countLessThan = (s) -> {
            int n = s.length();
            int[][] dp = new int[n + 1][b + 1];
            for (int i = 0; i <= n; i++) {
                for (int j = 0; j <= b; j++) {
                    dp[i][j] = 0;
                }
            }

            for (int i = 0; i <= b; i++) {
                dp[1][i] = 1;
            }

            for (int i = 2; i <= n; i++) {
                for (int d = 0; d <= b; d++) {
                    for (int prev = 0; prev <= d; prev++) {
                        dp[i][d] = (dp[i][d] + dp[i - 1][prev]) % MOD;
                    }
                }
            }

            int res = 0;
            for (int d = 1; d < s.charAt(0) - '0'; d++) {
                res = (res + dp[n][d]) % MOD;
            }

            int tight = s.charAt(0) - '0';
            for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
                int limit = s.charAt(i) - '0';
                for (int d = 0; d <= limit; d++) {
                    for (int prev = 0; prev <= d; prev++) {
                        res = (res + dp[n - i][prev]) % MOD;
                    }
                }
            }

            return res;
        };

        int total = countUpTo.apply(r);
        int lessThanL = countLessThan.apply(l);
        return (total - lessThanL + MOD) % MOD;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n * b^2), where n is the length of the input strings and b is the base
space complexity: O(n * b), for the DP table
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses dynamic programming to count the number of non-decreasing digit numbers up to a given number in base b. It calculates the count for the upper bound and subtracts the count for the lower bound to get the result for the range [l, r]. The key strength is the efficient use of DP to avoid brute-force enumeration. The main weakness is the quadratic time complexity in base b, which could be a bottleneck for larger bases.
